title: iris pallida subsp. illyrica
intro: Iris pallida subsp. illyrica is a subspecies of the Iris pallida plant, commonly known as the Dalmatian iris, which is a type of flowering plant in the iris family. It is a perennial plant native to the Balkan Peninsula, known for its beautiful purple flowers and medicinal properties. The root of the Iris pallida subsp. illyrica plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory problems, due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's medicinal properties can be harnessed through herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to promote health and well-being, including relieving coughs, sore throats, and skin conditions.
